mainEntity.name: How do I get my local business to show up when people use Cortana voice search nearby (e.g., in Los Angeles or San Diego)?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Cortana gets its local business answers from Bing search, Bing Places, and structured data, so cleaning up those sources is the fastest way to show up.

If Cortana misreads or ignores your business, your listings and on‑page details are probably incomplete or inconsistent.

**Key factors**
- Fully claim and optimize Bing Places for Business, including categories and hours
- Keep NAP (name, address, phone) consistent across major directories
- Use LocalBusiness schema on your site, with accurate address and geo
- Encourage reviews and clear, answer‑style content on key service pages

In Los Angeles or San Diego, where competition is high, small errors in Bing Places (wrong category, missing suite number) are often enough to push your business out of Cortana’s spoken answers. Audit every place your address appears.

Treat Cortana local visibility as the downstream result of strong, consistent local SEO data.
acceptedAnswer.description: To show up in Cortana’s local voice answers, fully optimize and correct your Bing Places listing, keep your business name, address, and phone consistent across directories, add LocalBusiness schema to your site, and write clear, answer‑style content. Cortana leans heavily on Bing for local results.
